data.title: Enact Holdings targets $350M shareholder returns in 2025 supported by robust financial performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Enact Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:ACT) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Rohit Gupta highlighted 2024 as a record year for Enact Holdings, with adjusted operating income reaching $718 million, a 9% year-over-year increase. This was attributed to strong credit performance, operational efficiencies, and resilient capital allocation strategies, including $750 million in senior notes issuance, which reduced annual interest expense by $2 million.

  • Adjusted return on equity for 2024 was 15%, and adjusted book value per share rose by 12% year-over-year to $34.16. Gupta emphasized the company’s commitment to disciplined risk management and expense control while achieving $354 million in capital returns to shareholders.

  • CFO Dean Mitchell reported Q4 adjusted operating income of $169 million, up 7% year-over-year, with adjusted EPS at $1.09. Primary insurance in force reached a record $269 billion, supported by $13 billion in new insurance written during the quarter, reflecting a 27% year-over-year increase.

Outlook

  • Management guided capital returns for 2025 to be aligned with the $350 million achieved in 2024. This includes quarterly dividends and share buybacks, with flexibility for adjustments based on business performance and market conditions.

  • CEO Gupta noted a strong long-term outlook for the U.S. housing market, supported by robust demographic drivers and low inventory levels. Pricing in the mortgage insurance market remains constructive, and the company plans to maintain its risk-adjusted pricing strategy.

  • Expense guidance for 2025 is set at $220 million to $225 million, reflecting continued investments in growth initiatives and modernization while managing inflationary pressures.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted operating income for Q4 2024 was $169 million, compared to $182 million in Q3 2024. Adjusted EPS stood at $1.09, slightly below the $1.16 reported in the previous quarter. Persistency declined to 82% in Q4 from 83% in Q3.

  • Total net premiums earned for Q4 were $246 million, down 1% sequentially but up 2% year-over-year, driven by primary insurance in force growth. Investment income rose 12% year-over-year to $63 million, benefiting from elevated interest rates and a resilient A-rated portfolio.

  • Losses increased to $24 million in Q4, compared to $12 million in Q3, reflecting lower reserve releases and new delinquencies. The reserve release for the quarter was $56 million, driven by strong cure performance.

Q&A

  • Bose George, KBW, inquired about capital return flexibility in a high-rate environment. CFO Dean Mitchell explained that while the $350 million guidance for 2025 remains firm, adjustments could be made midyear based on business and macroeconomic conditions.

  • Doug Harter, UBS, queried about the impact of seasoning on the portfolio's delinquency trends. CFO Mitchell clarified that while delinquency rates may plateau as books season, embedded equity in the portfolio supports strong cure rates.

  • Analysts raised questions about the performance and growth potential of Enact Re. CEO Gupta emphasized the long-term growth strategy for Enact Re, highlighting its participation in GSE CRT transactions and attractive risk-adjusted returns.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on capital return strategies and portfolio performance. Concerns were raised about potential macroeconomic impacts on credit quality and delinquency trends.

  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing strong financial results and disciplined execution. However, the Q&A responses were measured, with CEO Gupta reiterating cautious optimism about market conditions and regulatory changes.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, with a focus on resilience and long-term growth opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 adjusted operating income declined slightly from Q3, while adjusted EPS also fell from $1.16 to $1.09. Persistency saw a one-point decrease sequentially.

  • New insurance written in Q4 was $13 billion, down sequentially from $14 billion in Q3 due to seasonality. Investment income, however, increased by $2 million quarter-over-quarter.

  • Losses rose significantly in Q4, with the loss ratio increasing to 10% from 5% in Q3, driven by hurricane-related delinquencies and lower reserve releases.

  • Analysts' focus shifted more toward capital return strategies and the impact of macroeconomic conditions on credit performance, compared to competitive dynamics and pricing discussed in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted new delinquencies, which rose by 6% in Q4 due to hurricane-related impacts. Excluding hurricanes, new delinquencies increased by 1%, consistent with seasonal trends.

  • The regulatory environment remains a key area of focus, with potential changes under the new administration that could impact the broader mortgage insurance market.

  • Analysts expressed concerns about the seasoning of newer books and its impact on delinquency trends, as well as the potential for macroeconomic headwinds to affect credit performance.

Final Takeaway

Enact Holdings delivered strong financial results in Q4 2024, supported by record insurance in force and disciplined expense management. Management reiterated its commitment to returning $350 million to shareholders in 2025 while navigating potential macroeconomic and regulatory challenges. The company remains optimistic about long-term housing market trends and plans to leverage its robust capital position and risk-adjusted pricing strategy to drive sustained growth and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Cardinal Health raised to buy by Jefferies
data.description:
Jefferies upgraded Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH) to buy from hold, citing management's "impressive" EPS track record.

The investment bank said that after ten quarters it believes the company's earnings track record "is undeniable and clearly differentiated" compared to its earlier history.

"We think management has laid the foundation to continue strong growth trends by adding strategic assets which should boost CAH's exposure to Spec Rx growth opportunity," Jefferies added.

Jefferies views the company's 2H25 guidance as conservative, adding that Cardinal's (NYSE:CAH) acquisition of ADS "should provide a modest lift to 2H25 when closed."

The investment bank also sees Cardinal (CAH) guiding for earnings growth of 10% to 14% when it holds its annual Investor Day in June.

Jefferies raised its price target for the stock to $150 from $140.

data.title: Digi International targets $200M ARR by 2028, reports record $120M ARR in Q1 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Digi International Inc. (NASDAQ:DGII) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ron Konezny highlighted that Digi International began the second year of its five-year goal to double annual recurring revenue (ARR) and adjusted EBITDA to $200M each. ARR reached a record $120M, an 11% increase year-over-year and $4M higher than the previous quarter. ARR now represents 28% of total quarterly revenues, reflecting strong performance across both reporting segments.

  • Konezny noted the company's focus on reducing debt, with total outstanding debt now below $100M for the first time since fiscal Q4 2021. Digi generated $30M in cash from operations during the quarter and aims to retire all debt by the end of calendar 2025.

  • The company plans to leverage its improved balance sheet for solution-oriented acquisitions and remains committed to addressing evolving geopolitical challenges, including tariff policies, through its geographically diverse manufacturing base.

  • CFO Jamie Loch stated, "We generated $30 million in cash from operations in the quarter and further reduced inventory balances."

Outlook

  • Management maintained confidence in achieving its five-year goal of $200M in ARR and adjusted EBITDA by 2028. Konezny emphasized that ARR growth remains the top strategic priority.

  • Guidance for fiscal Q2 2025 suggests flat sequential revenue and a slight decline in EBITDA due to an expected normalization of gross margins following favorable product mix in Q1. Loch explained that gross margins at 60% or better would remain a focus, but Q1's favorable mix is unlikely to repeat at the same scale in Q2.

  • Konezny highlighted opportunities from infrastructure investments and increasing demand for IoT solutions, particularly among Western companies favoring localized providers.

Financial Results

  • Digi International reported Q1 2025 ARR of $120M, representing an 11% increase year-over-year. ARR growth was supported by both the product and service business segments.

  • Total outstanding debt dropped below $100M, aided by $30M in cash generated from operations. The company reiterated plans to be net debt-free by the end of calendar 2025.

  • Gross margins exceeded 60%, benefiting from a favorable product mix and recurring revenue growth. Management noted that ARR's increasing share of the revenue mix would continue to support gross margins.

Q&A

  • Tommy Moll, Stephens, asked about the drivers behind ARR growth and the $4.7M decline in one-time revenue. CFO Loch explained that the transition from one-time revenue to recurring revenue is part of the strategic path, with noticeable yet manageable shifts in revenue composition.

  • James Fish, Piper Sandler, inquired about the impact of potential tariff changes and demand dynamics. Konezny noted that over 70% of revenues come from North America and emphasized the company’s geographically diversified manufacturing base, which includes U.S. facilities to mitigate tariff risks.

  • Josh Nichols, B. Riley Securities, questioned customer ordering patterns and end-market performance. Konezny observed stabilized or modestly improved ordering patterns and highlighted strong demand in data centers, medical devices, and utilities.

  • Nichols also asked about attach rates for ARR. Konezny revealed over 50% attach rates for key products, with higher figures in segments like cellular routers, driven by strategic efforts and new leadership in the Opengear division.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on ARR growth and its implications for long-term profitability. Questions highlighted revenue composition, tariff risks, and end-market dynamics.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly in discussing progress toward long-term goals and ARR growth. Phrases like "we remain as confident as ever" and "we are very optimistic" underscored their positive outlook.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed a consistent focus on ARR and recurring revenue, with management demonstrating increased confidence in operational resilience.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • ARR grew from $116M in Q4 2024 to $120M in Q1 2025, marking an $4M sequential increase. ARR's share of total revenue also increased from 27% to 28%.

  • Debt reduction accelerated, with outstanding debt falling below $100M compared to prior quarter levels. Cash generation from operations strengthened, reflecting improved inventory management.

  • Management tone remained confident, with an emphasis on long-term strategic goals. Analysts continued to focus on recurring revenue trends and potential macroeconomic impacts.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted uncertainties related to geopolitical factors and tariff policies. Konezny outlined mitigation strategies, including a diversified manufacturing footprint and plans to finalize the shift away from China.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential impact of tariff changes on costs and demand, as well as the sustainability of ARR growth amid macroeconomic headwinds.

Final Takeaway

Digi International delivered a strong Q1 2025 performance, highlighted by record ARR of $120M and significant debt reduction. Management remains focused on its five-year goals of $200M ARR and adjusted EBITDA, supported by recurring revenue growth and a diversified operational strategy. While analysts raised questions about potential tariff impacts and revenue composition, management expressed confidence in its ability to navigate challenges and capitalize on market opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Align Technology projects 2025 mid-single-digit volume growth for clear aligners amid new product launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Joe Hogan highlighted Q4 2024 revenue of $995 million, reflecting 4% year-over-year growth. Clear aligner volumes increased 6.1% year-over-year, with strong performance in EMEA, APAC, and Latin America, offset by seasonal softness in North America.
  • Clear aligner ASPs declined, attributed to unfavorable foreign exchange impacts and product mix shifts. Hogan emphasized the successful rollout of the Invisalign Palatal Expander system, which gained regulatory approvals in Europe and the UK, aiming to enhance adoption in 2025.
  • CFO John Morici noted that operating margins exceeded the Q4 outlook, with non-GAAP operating margins at 23.2%. He also highlighted a $30 million equity investment in Smile Doctors, reinforcing Align’s partnership with DSOs.

Outlook

  • Align anticipates Q1 2025 revenue between $965 million and $985 million, reflecting seasonal softness and forex impacts. Full-year 2025 revenue growth is projected in the low single digits, with mid-single-digit growth expected in clear aligner volumes.
  • Management expects 2025 GAAP operating margins to improve by approximately 2 points, supported by restructuring efforts and scaling of direct 3D printing manufacturing.
  • Clear aligner ASPs are projected to decline year-over-year in 2025 due to unfavorable forex and a product mix shift toward non-comprehensive aligners.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 adjusted EPS was $2.44, exceeding analysts’ estimates of $2.45. Revenues reached $995.2 million, slightly below the $999.2 million estimate but up sequentially and year-over-year.
  • Systems and Services revenue grew 14.9% year-over-year, with increased scanner volumes and adoption of the iTero Lumina scanner. Clear aligner revenue rose 1.6% year-over-year, driven by higher volumes but offset by price and mix shifts.
  • Free cash flow for Q4 stood at $263 million, with significant investments in manufacturing capacity and facilities.

Q&A

  • Michael Cherny, Leerink Partners, asked about clear aligner guidance dynamics. CFO John Morici confirmed mid-single-digit volume growth expectations for 2025, driven by stable demand trends across regions.
  • Elizabeth Anderson, Evercore ISI, inquired about the iTero Lumina scanner’s restorative software launch. CEO Hogan highlighted the product’s positive reception and anticipated broader adoption in Q2 2025.
  • Jon Block, Stifel, questioned competitive dynamics in the U.S. market. Hogan noted stable competition but emphasized macroeconomic challenges affecting orthodontic practices and patient conversion rates.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about U.S. consumer sentiment and its impact on orthodontic demand. Multiple questions focused on ASP pressures, forex impacts, and macroeconomic challenges.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing innovative product launches and international growth. Hogan’s prepared remarks reflected confidence in long-term growth despite near-term headwinds.
  • Compared to Q3, analysts’ tone was more skeptical regarding U.S. market recovery, while management’s tone remained consistent, focusing on strategic initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue grew sequentially by 1.8%, supported by stronger Systems and Services performance. Clear aligner volumes rebounded in EMEA and APAC but faced seasonal declines in North America.
  • Management’s tone showed increased emphasis on international growth and new product launches, similar to Q3, but with heightened caution regarding forex impacts.
  • Analysts continued to probe ASP dynamics and macroeconomic challenges, with recurring concerns over U.S. market softness.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged forex volatility as a significant risk, impacting clear aligner ASPs and operating margins.
  • Analysts raised concerns about continued sluggishness in the U.S. orthodontic market and its potential to constrain growth.
  • Potential tariffs on Mexico-to-U.S. shipments were noted as a cost risk, estimated at $4 million to $5 million per month if implemented.

Final Takeaway

Align Technology reported solid Q4 results, with year-over-year revenue and volume growth, driven by international markets. Management provided cautious guidance for 2025, anticipating mid-single-digit clear aligner volume growth and low single-digit revenue growth, supported by new product launches and operational efficiencies. Forex impacts and U.S. market challenges remain key risks, but investments in innovation and partnerships with DSOs position the company for long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gold sets another record as trade war turmoil adds to safe-haven demand
data.description:
Gold futures settled Wednesday at yet another new record, the third consecutive session that the metal gained and the sixth positive close in the past seven sessions, driven by investors seeking a safe haven with the threat of escalating tariffs, as well as accelerating purchases from central banks.

Central banks continued to buy gold "at an eye-watering pace" in 2024, according to a report by the World Gold Council, as total demand last year reached a new high of 4,974 metric tons.

Central bank purchases were driven by "concerns about ongoing inflation, geopolitical tensions, and needs to add diversification to their portfolios," according to WGC strategist Joe Cavatoni.

The metal is gaining on a weaker U.S. dollar and growing concerns of an escalating U.S.-China trade war, and the likely hit to global economic growth prospects, ActivTrades analyst Ricardo Evangelista said.

The weaker dollar follows President Trump's U-turn on imposing additional tariffs on Mexico and Canada, and disappointing U.S. labor and industrial data, Evangelista wrote, and U.S. tariffs on China and China's response are ominous for the global economy, increasing gold's safe-haven appeal.

Demand is reinforced by further Middle East uncertainty, after Trump's comments about taking over Gaza and displacing the Palestinian population, Evangelista added.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for February delivery closed +0.6% to $2,871.60/oz, while front-month February silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -0.1% to $32.857/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

There are indications of increased demand for gold as major dealers seek to shift metal to the U.S. before any tariffs are imposed, Bloomberg reported.

One month lease rates in London have jumped to ~4.7%, far above previous levels of near-zero, according to the report; the rate reflects the return that holders of bullion in London's vaults can get by loaning their metal out to other buyers on a short-term basis.

Bloomberg said last week that the rush for gold has led to weeks-long queues to withdraw bullion from the Bank of England - where many central banks around the world hold reserves - to deposit with private banks; gold also has been flowing into depositories of New York's Comex exchange.

The flows have added to tightness in the market, according to Rhona O’Connell, head of market analysis for EMEA and Asia at StoneX Group.

data.title: Kirkland's closes $25M Beyond investment
data.description:
Shares of Kirkland’s (NASDAQ:KIRK) were driven more than 6% higher after the close on Wednesday after the company said Beyond (NYSE:BYON) has completed both an $8 million equity purchase under a subscription agreement and the mandatory conversion of an $8.5 million convertible term loan under a term loan credit agreement.

With the completion of this transaction, Beyond (NYSE:BYON) has now provided Kirkland's (NASDAQ:KIRK) with a total of $25M of capital and now owns ~40% of Kirkland's (NASDAQ:KIRK) outstanding shares of common stock.

"Today marks a pivotal moment for Kirkland's, as the completion of this transaction and ongoing value of our strategic partnership with Beyond begins to unlock new drivers of transformation following our efforts over the past year focused on revitalizing the Kirkland's brand,” said CEO Amy Sulivan.

Last October, Kirkland’s (KIRK) and Beyond (NYSE:BYON), the parent company of Bed Bath & Beyond, agreed to a partnership that would increase the exposure of Bed Bath & Beyond by offering its merchandise in Kirkland’s (KIRK) brick and mortar locations. Kirkland’s (KIRK) will also be the licensee for new, smaller format neighborhood Bed Bath & Beyond locations nationwide.

The collaboration is for a seven-year term during which Beyond (BYON) will earn a fee of 0.25% of Kirkland’s quarterly retail and e-commerce revenue beginning FQ1 2025, along with an incentive fee of equal to 1.5% of Kirkland’s (KIRK) incremental growth in e-commerce revenue during the term of the agreement. Beyond (BYON) will earn a store royalty fee equal to 3% of net store sales generated under the Bed Bath & Beyond banner, increasing to 5% after the agreement expires and if locations are still operating.

The agreement also gives Beyond (BYON) the right to nominate two directors to the Kirkland’s (KIRK) board as long as Beyond (BYON) retains at least a 20% stake in Kirkland’s (KIRK). This is reduced to one director if Beyond’s (BYON) stake falls to at least 5%.

data.title: Digital Turbine projects nearly 50% EBITDA growth for Q4 2025 amid strong international on-device performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Digital Turbine, Inc. (NASDAQ:APPS) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bill Stone announced the appointment of Steve Lasher as the new CFO, effective immediately, while long-standing CFO Barrett Garrison transitions to a consultant role. Lasher's prior experience includes leadership roles at Vonage and IBM.
  • Revenue for the quarter reached $135 million, adjusted EBITDA was $22 million, and non-GAAP EPS was $0.13. Stone attributed the performance to improved advertising demand, progress on transformation initiatives, and strong execution, especially for the international on-device business.
  • On-device international revenues grew 100% year-over-year, driven by heightened advertiser demand and operational improvements. Revenue per device (RPD) reached new record levels both in the U.S. and internationally, though U.S. device volumes remained soft.
  • The app growth platform (AGP) segment delivered $44 million in revenue, supported by a 34% year-over-year increase in brand-focused revenues. This growth is attributed to leveraging first-party data and diversifying beyond gaming applications into non-gaming.
  • CFO Barrett Garrison confirmed that the company is on track to achieve $25 million in annualized operating expense savings, aligning cost structures to enhance profitability and efficiency.

Outlook

  • The company raised its guidance for the March quarter, projecting year-over-year growth across revenue and nearly 50% growth in adjusted EBITDA.
  • Key growth drivers include expanding the device footprint, particularly internationally, scaling new products, and enhancing relationships with media and brands.
  • Management highlighted the strategic importance of SDK bidding, which now accounts for 70% of total impressions on its exchange, compared to 5% a year ago.

Financial Results

  • Revenue of $134.6 million marked a 13% sequential increase, while adjusted EBITDA reached $22 million. Free cash flow for the quarter was $6.4 million.
  • The on-device solutions (ODS) segment generated $91.7 million in revenue, reflecting an 11% sequential increase, despite a 3% year-over-year decline due to softer U.S. device volumes.
  • The AGP segment delivered $43.8 million in revenue, with brand-focused revenues growing 34% year-over-year. Non-gaming application revenues nearly doubled over the past year, diversifying beyond gaming.
  • Gross margin declined slightly to 44%, compared to 45% in the previous quarter, due to product mix changes.
  • Cash operating expenses declined 3% sequentially, reflecting cost-saving measures under the transformation program.

Q&A

  • Anthony Stoss, Craig-Hallum: Questioned the resurgence of major brands in advertising and the role of seasonality. CEO Bill Stone attributed the improvement to consistent efforts in building relationships and trust with brands like P&G, Disney, and Starbucks.
  • Stoss also asked about the adoption of alternative app stores. Stone acknowledged that Epic and Microsoft’s initiatives have built market awareness and emphasized the importance of regulatory developments in the European Union for potential growth in 2025.
  • Arthur Chu, BofA Securities: Inquired about key growth drivers for fiscal 2025. Stone highlighted the importance of leveraging first-party data, expanding device partnerships, and scaling brand relationships, stating that the company aims to sustain its current momentum.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the sustainability of recent growth drivers like brand advertising and alternative app distribution. Questions reflected a mix of curiosity and skepticism, particularly regarding the competitive landscape and device sales recovery.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing successful execution and future growth opportunities. CEO Stone frequently referenced progress in transformation initiatives and expressed optimism about the company’s strategic positioning.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone was markedly more positive, with both management and analysts acknowledging tangible improvements in key performance areas.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted positively, with management projecting significant EBITDA growth for Q4 compared to marginal expectations in Q2.
  • International on-device revenue growth accelerated from 25% in Q2 to 100% year-over-year in Q3, driven by strong advertiser demand.
  • Analysts in Q3 focused more on brand adoption and regulatory opportunities compared to concerns about U.S. device sales and legacy business declines in Q2.
  • Management confidence improved notably, bolstered by operational efficiencies and strong execution on strategic initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Continued softness in U.S. device volumes remains a challenge, though management anticipates stabilization in 2025.
  • Legacy performance DSP declines represent a key area requiring improvement, with management emphasizing the need to leverage first-party data to enhance performance.
  • Regulatory and competitive dynamics in alternative app distribution could affect growth trajectories, particularly in regions like the EU.

Final Takeaway

Digital Turbine demonstrated significant progress in Q3 2025, driven by strong international on-device performance and growth in brand revenues. The company’s transformation initiatives are yielding cost savings, while strategic investments in first-party data and SDK bidding are positioning it for sustained growth. With nearly 50% projected EBITDA growth for Q4, management’s focus on efficiency and innovation suggests a positive outlook despite challenges in U.S. device volumes and legacy DSP transitions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Becton, Dickinson confirms plans to separate life sciences business
data.description:
• Confirming a news report from Feb. 3, Becton, Dickinson (NYSE:BDX) on Wednesday said its board approved a plan to separate its Biosciences and Diagnostic Solutions business from the rest of the company.
  • "We believe the separation will position New BD as a differentiated MedTech leader and enable optimized investment to accelerate our innovation pipeline and ongoing margin enhancement through BD Excellence to further fund growth," CEO, Chairman, and President Tom Polen said in a statement.
  • "We believe Biosciences and Diagnostic Solutions is expected to deliver substantial value as a pure-play leader in life sciences and will be well-positioned to execute on its unique and compelling solutions and growth opportunities."
  • BD believes the separation will unlock value in two ways. The New BD will be a growth-oriented MedTech with four segments: medical essentials; connected care; biopharma systems; and interventional. The Biosciences and Diagnostic Solutions business is expected to become a leader in life sciences tools and diagnostics.
